sections.paragraph:
ἄτρεπτος, ον (s. τρέπω; Chrysipp.+ [Stoic. II 158]; Plut.; Aelian; Herm. Wr.; IG IX/2, 317, 2; Job 15:15 Sym.; Philo, Leg. All. 1, 51 al.; Jos., Ant. 11, 57; τὸν ἄ. … θεόν Just., A I, 13, 4) unchangeable δόξα IEph ins.—DELG s.v. τρέπω.

Chrysipp Chrysipp , phil., III b.c.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 149
